group_works.title: Takahide Saita: shipyard foreman
group_works.creator_credit: Nippon Koho Center

results.title: Takahide Saita: shipyard foreman
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Faces of Japan”
Focuses on the life of a shipyard worker in Tokyo, his work on ship construction, his position within the company, his relationship to his workers and his family life.

results.title: Atsushi Chiba: mechanical engineer
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Faces of Japan”.
Atsushi Chiba is a 30 year old engineer at an auto-parts factory north of Tokyo. This documentary focuses on the technical and social aspects of his working environment and touches on attitudes and aspirations in his family life.

results.title: The Enlightenment
results.media_note: Sorry, we don't have images or video for this item.

group_works.title: The Marriage of Figaro
group_works.creator_credit: Open University

results.title: The Marriage of Figaro
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Enlightenment”.
Consists of three scenes from Beaumarchais’ play “The Marriage of Figaro” and the same scenes from Mozart’s opera. The following scenes are performed - Act V. Part of Figaro’s monologue and part of Figaro’s aria. Act III. The duet between the Count and Susanna. Act II. Part of the scene in which Susanna is discovered in the Countess’s dressing room.(the beginning of the finale of Act II of the opera).

group_works.title: The Electronic Office
group_works.creator_credit: BBC-TV

results.title: The Electronic Office
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “The Electronic Office”.
A computer education program that consists of six parts, each 25 minutes long. The parts are: 1.”Office on the move”, 2.”The stand-alone micro”, 3. “Wiring up the organisation”, 4. “Changing with the times”, 5. “Wiring up the world”, 6.”Easy to use”.

results.title: Water for dry land
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Earth and it’s peoples”
The dependence of man upon his supply of water. The transformation of desert lands by controlling water supply is shown.

results.title: Tropical lowland: the Amazon River, Brazil
results.creator_credit: Jules Bucher and Louis de Rochemont Associates
results.brief_description:
Episode of series “The Earth and its peoples”
The Amazon River drains two-fifths of the continent of South America and varies in width from a mile to 800 miles at its mouth. Rain falls nearly every day, the climate hot and humid and the rain forest is almost impenetrable. Travel is almost entirely by water. Products are rosewood, brazil nuts, rubber, jute, cocoa. We are shown a family living in primitive conditions and the effect an unhealthy climate can have upon people and their development.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Jules Bucher and Louis de Rochemont Associates
